sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच
इत्यर्जुनं वासुदेवस्तथोक्त्वा स्वकं रूपं दर्शयामास भूयः।
आश्वासयामास च भीतमेनं भूत्वा पुनः सौम्यवपुर्महात्मा॥
transliteration:
sañjaya uvāca
ity arjunaṁ vāsudevas tathoktvā svakaṁ rūpaṁ darśayām āsa bhūyaḥ|
āśvāsayām āsa ca bhītam enaṁ bhūtvā punaḥ saumya-vapuḥ mahātmā||
literal_meaning: Sañjaya said: Having thus spoken to Arjuna, Vāsudeva again showed His own form, and the Great-souled One, assuming once more His gentle (beautiful) form, consoled the frightened one.
contextual_meaning: After the terrifying cosmic vision, Kṛṣṇa reverts to a familiar, comforting form to regulate Arjuna’s overstimulated nervous system and re-establish felt safety.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic soothing: the psyche is brought back from hyper-arousal to secure base through a trusted attachment figure.
trigger_scenario: A client who has just re-experienced a traumatic memory in therapy is guided back to the present by the therapist’s calm voice and familiar office surroundings.
anchor_text: God returns to a gentle form to calm your fear.
